join_order.notes: Scan c_mktsegment + c_custkey; build dense bool bitset[1500001] over c_custkey. ~1.5 MB, fully L3-resident. Probe o_custkey via O(1) direct index. Customer scan is small enough (~7 MB total) to run single-threaded before parallel orders scan.

Filter orders: cust_bitset[o_custkey] AND o_orderdate < 9204 (~20% × 48.7% = ~9.74% selectivity, ~1.46M rows). Build two structures: (1) qualifying_orders_bitset[60000001] — 7.5 MB, L3-resident prefilter; (2) compact_hash_map<int32_t,OrderMeta> pre-sized to 1.46M entries with {orderdate, shippriority}. Lineitem probe uses zone map to skip blocks (max_date <= 9204), then shipdate filter, then bitset prefilter (~82% early reject), then hash map lookup for metadata.

filters.notes: lineitem sorted ASC by l_shipdate; ~273 of 600 blocks skipped entirely
~1.46M qualifying orders × avg 4 lineitems/order; bitset prefilter rejects ~82% cheaply before hash map lookup

data_structures.customer_filter: bitset — bool cust_ok[1500001], zero-initialized, 1.5 MB, L3-resident
data_structures.orders_prefilter: bitset — bool qualifying_orders[60000001], zero-initialized, 60 MB allocated but only ~7.5 MB of actual bits used; L3-resident working set for probed keys
data_structures.orders_metadata: compact_hash_map(pre_sized=2000000) — unordered_map<int32_t, OrderMeta> where OrderMeta = {int32_t orderdate; int32_t shippriority}; keyed by o_orderkey
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local unordered_map<int32_t, AggState> pre_sized=500000 per thread — AggState = {double revenue; int32_t orderdate; int32_t shippriority}; keyed by l_orderkey (uniquely determines orderdate+shippriority post-join); parallel merge into global map after scan

phase_breakdown.phase1_customer: single_threaded — 7 MB total I/O (c_mktsegment 1.4 MB + c_custkey 5.7 MB); sequential scan, build cust_ok bitset
phase_breakdown.phase2_orders: parallel — 228 MB total I/O across 4 columns; each thread scans a row range, applies cust_ok + orderdate filters, appends to thread-local vector<OrderRow>; main thread merges into shared qualifying_orders bitset + hash map
phase_breakdown.phase3_lineitem: morsel_driven parallel — zone-map block skipping applied per-thread; each thread processes morsels of ~100k rows (full block); thread-local agg hash maps; parallel reduce/merge at end

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks — skip lineitem blocks where zones[b].max_date <= 9204; eliminates ~273/600 blocks (~45.5% of lineitem data)

not_used — 240 MB random-access array avoided in favor of in-memory qualifying_orders bitset + hash map built from filtered orders; avoids random access penalty into 240 MB structure during hot lineitem scan
not_used — direct customer column scan with bitset build is cheaper than join-index indirection for a pure filter step

aggregation: hash_group_by — keyed by l_orderkey only (l_orderkey uniquely identifies o_orderdate and o_shippriority after join); AggState stores {revenue (double), orderdate (int32_t), shippriority (int32_t)}; thread-local maps merged via lock-free partition or sequential merge

output.strategy: partial_sort (nth_element + sort on top-10 candidates from merged agg map)
